methods.description: The process of acquiring Raman spectra and images from prepared pollen grains using a confocal Raman microscope with specific settings.

steps.description: The Raman measurements were performed on a confocal Raman microscope alpha300 R (WITec, Ulm, Germany) equipped with a 785 nm narrow-band diode laser (Toptica Photonics, Gräfelingen, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Set up and operate confocal Raman microscope with 785 nm laser.

steps.description: The spectrometer, equipped a 300 g/mm grating, allows us to cover the wavenumber region between 100 to 3300 cm −1 at a spectral resolution of approx. 6 cm −1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Configure spectrometer grating and spectral range.
outputs: Spectrometer configured for specific range/resolution (100-3300 cm⁻¹, ~6 cm⁻¹ resolution)

steps.description: Raman spectra were recorded with an integration time of 0.5 s using a 50× Zeiss objective with 0.9 NA.
steps.searchable_summary: Set acquisition parameters (integration time, objective) for Raman spectra recording.

steps.description: The prepared pollen grains were placed on a CaF 2 slide.
steps.searchable_summary: Mount prepared pollen sample on a CaF2 slide.

steps.description: and the Raman images were recorded on individual grains with a step size of 1 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: Record Raman images by scanning individual pollen grains with a 1 µm step size.

methods.description: Analysis workflow for processing and interpreting Raman hyperspectral images of pollen grains, including preprocessing, normalization, outlier removal, smoothing, Hierarchical Cluster Analysis (HCA), and Multivariate Curve Resolution - Alternating Least Squares (MCR-ALS).

steps.description: The data analysis of the recorded Raman images was performed in R with hyperSpec as a main package [44,45].
steps.searchable_summary: Initialize data analysis environment using R and hyperSpec package.

steps.description: Several preprocessing steps were performed, including wavenumber calibration, with paracetamol spectra as references, and interpolation.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform wavenumber calibration using reference spectra and interpolate data.

steps.description: The rubberband method was used to correct the background.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply rubberband method for background correction of spectra.

steps.description: and afterwards a cosmic spike correction algorithm [46] was used.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply cosmic spike correction algorithm to spectra.

steps.description: An area normalization over the range of 400 to 3100 cm −1 was implemented.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform area normalization on spectra over a specified wavenumber range (400-3100 cm⁻¹).

steps.description: In order to reduce the dataset, spectra not exhibiting information from the pollen grains were removed, e.g., of the surrounding CaF 2 slide.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter and remove spectra corresponding to background or non-sample areas (e.g., CaF2 slide).
outputs: Filtered dataset containing only sample spectra

steps.description: and a correlation function with a threshold of 0.75 was implemented as a quality control to remove outliers.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove outlier spectra based on correlation threshold (0.75).

steps.description: A Savitzky-Golay function from package parcma was chosen to smooth the spectra with a quadratic filter order and filter length of 9 [47].
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Savitzky-Golay smoothing filter (quadratic order, length 9) to spectra.

steps.description: An HCA based on a Pearson distance calculation and the Ward clustering algorithm of the entire dataset grouped the spectra into clusters of the same spectral profile.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Hierarchical Cluster Analysis (HCA) using Pearson distance and Ward linkage on the dataset.

steps.description: In a false-color representation, the calculated weighted mean spectra of the clusters can be presented in the imaging domain, revealing spectral differences within one pollen grain.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate weighted mean spectra for each cluster and visualize cluster distribution in a false-color image.

steps.description: Additionally, an MCR-ALS algorithm was chosen to calculate the pure components and their abundances in a quantitative approach [41].
steps.searchable_summary: Apply Multivariate Curve Resolution - Alternating Least Squares (MCR-ALS) to decompose the dataset into pure components and their concentration profiles.

steps.description: In an alternating fashion, pure spectra and concentration profiles were optimized until a convergence criterion was achieved [42].
steps.searchable_summary: Optimize MCR-ALS model iteratively by alternating optimization of pure spectra and concentration profiles until convergence.

steps.description: The five cluster mean spectra from the HCA were used as input starting spectra.
steps.searchable_summary: Use the five mean spectra from HCA as initial estimates for pure component spectra in MCR-ALS.
outputs: Initial pure component spectra for MCR-ALS

steps.description: and non-negativity constraints for both the spectra and the concentrations were set.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply non-negativity constraints to both pure component spectra and concentration profiles during MCR-ALS optimization.
